assertions.description: uses editor.addPipe() (not editor.on(), which is Rete v1 API)

assertions.evidence: if (context.type !== 'connectioncreate') return context; — early return guards the rest correctly

assertions.description: returns undefined (not false or throw) to cancel the connection
assertions.evidence: return undefined; — comment: 'Returning undefined drops the event, preventing the connection.'

assertions.description: calls editor.getNode() to get node instances for type checking
assertions.evidence: editor.getNode(source) and editor.getNode(target) used to retrieve node instances

assertions.description: returns context to permit valid connections through
assertions.evidence: return context; — after all type-check branches

notes: Perfect score — draw with with_skill. Pipe validation semantics (addPipe, undefined=block, context=allow, getNode) are well-documented in the public Rete.js docs. This eval tests an area where the baseline already performs well.
